answer: The term refers to a family of knots used to bind a rope to an object, including another rope.
evidence: There is an intimate relationship between technique and creativity. Even if single column ties and frictions are enough to create an infinite amount of tie variations, it is great to have a few more knots in your toolbox. Hitches are a family of knots used to bind a rope to an object, including another rope and are quite handy to design new ties.
expected_answer: a family of knots used to bind a rope to an object, including another rope

answer: The cuff locks in only one direction, and pulling the origin rope may tighten it.
evidence: You can use a cow hitch to make a locked cuff, slightly more secure than the half hitch inline cuff from Day 12. The origin and exit rope cross each other inside the friction, this direction ensures the friction compacts if you are tying at an angle. Note that the cuff is only locked in one direction, if you pull on the origin rope, the cuff may tighten.
expected_answer: the cuff is only locked in one direction; if you pull on the origin rope, the cuff may tighten
